L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where dictionary keys from untrusted JSON input are not properly sanitized before being used in SQL queries. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through specially crafted key names in dictionary parameters, potentially leading to unauthorized data access or modification.

L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where dictionary keys from untrusted JSON input are not properly sanitized before being used in SQL queries. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through specially crafted key names in dictionary parameters, potentially leading to unauthorized data access or modification.

ground_truth.reason: The escape_dict function in PyMySQL escapes dictionary values but not dictionary keys, allowing SQL injection when untrusted JSON input with crafted keys is used as query parameters.
